h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.recoheredEarlier: So the downward arrow is Earth on the cart, not the cart moving down.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The rolling direction is not what fixes the label.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was expected to simply place the remaining card by reading the visible label, following the same rote 'object on the cart' form already established.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: puts the contact strip under the rightward arrow and moves the rolling-left strip into the side space
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: L3 physically sorts two sentence strips into distinct spaces using the interaction-vs-motion criterion, performing the classification device the tutor introduced rather than merely asserting understanding.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: T3 acknowledges the card-placement is insufficient to settle the motion-vs-interaction question L2 raised (stock-taking contrast) and introduces a new sorting task with physical sentence strips and a dedicated 'not a force label' space (new public mechanism), replacing the single-card placement routine with a two-category classification device.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The strip-sorting device is precisely fitted to L2's live pressure — the question of whether rolling direction affects the label — by giving the learner an actionable spatial criterion (two distinct spaces) that forces the interaction/motion distinction to be enacted rather than merely stated, though it stops short of a 5 because the tutor pre-labels which strip is which, limiting learner agency.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The card has named the contact; it has not yet tested the motion case. Sort the strips: under the arrow goes the sentence that names an interaction on the cart. The sentence about how the cart moves goes in the side space.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: T3 directly takes up L2's revised framing — that rolling direction might or might not fix the label — and converts it into a new sorting task, visibly changing the evidence standard from card-placement to sentence-classification in response to the learner's question.
